title: "The Making of an Investment Banker," With Jim Donovan
body:
Interview:
1. Read the WSJ every day. One article on macro economics, one on an especific company transaction, one on the op ed page.
2. Get familiar with the 3 financial statements.
3. Get the interviewer to talk about themself.
4. Ask open ended questions.

7:02 Keeping the job:
1. Find a role model. Whoever is the best.
2. Make it clear that you have no ego. No task is beneath you.
3. Take notes. It conveys seriousness, shows the importance you assign to the topic, gives confidence that you are not going to forget. Recap the meeting at the end.
4. Work hard.
5. First impressions die hard. Be very responsive, especially early on.
6. Never stop learning.

14:04 How to excell:
1. Give your client advise that is the best for them, even (and more so) if it's contrary to your interest.
2. Take a position. (Assume the responsability on the matter)
3. Embrace and relish in adversity.
4. Master discipline.
5. Excell on the merits.
6. Never, ever, give up. Don't take no for an answer.
author: @myfriedscallmeguido

title: "The Making of an Investment Banker," With Jim Donovan
body: I'm not and never going into investment banking. But my God I learned enough from this talk that I could apply to any business career. And it's more helpful than trying to get career advice at most undergraduate business programs. Jim Donovan's advice can be for anyone who wants to start learning business or be better at it.
author: @Dakid015

title: "The Making of an Investment Banker," With Jim Donovan
body: Love the bullet points he provides to navigate through not just a career in banking but in life as well. You don’t need tips on how to enjoy vacations or spend your money, like Bruce Lee said, don’t pray for an easy life, pray for the strength to endure a difficult one. In this case, Jim gives us the tools to navigate through a difficult but rewarding path.

title: "How to Read a Case" with UVA Law Professor Anne Coughlin
body:
As a law student I basically do the following:
- read the facts and determine what the issue is.
- read the end of the judgement to see what the court holds.
- then skim the judgement itself.

By doing this I can understand the context of the case and the reasoning process for the final decision.
author: @ZOOBZGOMEZ

title: "How to Read a Case" with UVA Law Professor Anne Coughlin
body:
As a law student in Germany, I fell in love with the way she speaks. Her teaching makes it easy to follow and understand! Her patience as well as her own passion about law is catching.
Thank you SO much for providing us this fantastic lecture! Lots of love and respect to this brilliant professor!
author: @lorie826

title: "How to Read a Case" with UVA Law Professor Anne Coughlin
body:
1:15 'close reading' and The doctrinal content of the case
1:58 'it'?
2:26 questions to ask when reading a case
2:47 'the doctrine that the case spells out'
3:03 the course curriculum
3:48 Cases teach a certain 'legal principle' or a certain 'doctrinal rule'
4:43
5:13 How does the law define the actus reus of attempt?
author: @Unknowledgeable1

title: "How to Read a Case" with UVA Law Professor Anne Coughlin
body:
Hey, y'all need to read the cases before class! She's too nice!

When I see that a case was recently done, I'm like, "Aw, shit. No one's gonna know this yet". When it's a case from the turn of the 20th century, I'm saying, "Yeah, this has been solidified, not changed, fantastic precedent". I won a settlement, without a lawyer, citing a couple of way old cases.

Fantastic lecture.

title: Sunscreen🧴For Oily Skin👍 | Honest Review✅ #shorts #sunscreen #sunscreenreview
body: Benzophenone-4 is safe to be used in cosmetics and personal care products. However, it can be mildly allergic in certain skin types. Therefore, a patch test is recommended before full application. If used above the prescribed percentage, Benzophenone-4 can also be toxic and bioaccumulative, linking it to cancer.

title: EVERYTHING You NEED to Know about VIRGINIA TECH
body:
Nice Vid man! I think I'm going to Virginia tech as a transfer student! VT is the only top School in VA (apart from W&M I believe) that has a Criminology/Criminal Justice degree and English literature which are two of the Majors I really want to study. I wanna be a Law Student and need the best most reputable school, with the best academic record to get into a really nice law School, I believe with the convenience of transfer, and the available Majors VT offers, It is perfect for me!

I live in NOVA, so the school is really far from home which is a bit scary ngl, but worth it. The school has a modern twist to Gothic architecture which I LOVE. Really excited to be going here! I am interested in STEM majors but for my career path I can't pursue it! I was thinking after Law School I could study some sort of stem major for my Doctoral Degree :)

title: UVA SORORITY RUSH VLOG *extremely honest* (I may have cried a lot lol)
body: One of my big college regrets was joining a sorority. They can be clique-y and cultish and a lil (read a lot) controlling. I know so many people who had great experiences, so many who had terrible ones, and so many with stories inbetween. Maybe the extra time to get to know them more will help you avoid even more heart break. Good luck, keep swimming.

author: @eva03254

title: UVA SORORITY RUSH VLOG *extremely honest* (I may have cried a lot lol)
body: I went through formal rush twice and it ended so bad for me! Thankfully, I found my home through cob and wouldn’t have it any other way❤ Now being on the other side of recruitment I see how messy the process is and there can be girls that we want but it’s a whole number game and one person’s vote can make or break if you come back. I remember we had a lot of great girls who we wanted but one vote changed everything and we missed out on a lot of girls who we loved! It definitely doesn’t define you as a person at all and the process is just very hard and tricky! Thank you for sharing your journey and how it’s not all happily ever after! I’m positive you’ll find your home one day whether it’s another sorority, a club, or just your friends

title: UVA SORORITY RUSH VLOG *extremely honest* (I may have cried a lot lol)
body:
I was in a sorority in college and i wouldn’t change it for anything because it taught me a lot, BUT it was also a source of major depression for me because I was legacy at a “top house” and I didn’t fit in AT ALL.
I’m curious to see if you’ll go through informal (probably soon, right?) but just know, you’ll have lasting memories and lasting friendships regardless ❤️ join clubs or teams if you can in the event you don’t end up in a sorority 🙂
author: @CrayolaCoffeeBean

title: UVA SORORITY RUSH VLOG *extremely honest* (I may have cried a lot lol)
body: Hi I wanted to let you know that I was in the same situation as you. My school only has one sorority so its less of a "find where you fit in" process and more of a "they like you or they don't process". Nevertheless I clicked with a lot of the girls in the sorority and I knew that I could find my home and my people there. I went through the rush process twice and both times I was rejected. The second time really hurt because I made it to preference night and they made me feel so loved just so they could turn me away the next day. Its been two years and theres still moments when I get FOMO after seeing pictures of all the fun stuff that they are doing. However, I found my home and my people in other places and I wouldn't trade my college experience for anything. I appreciate you sharing this video. I had such a hard time talking about how insecure that rejection made me because I did not want to come across as someone who was angry and bitter because they didn't get a bid. In order for sororities to make people feel special there has to be someone for them to feel superior to whether non-greek life people or other sororities. Keep your head held high. Keep putting yourself out there. It gets better with time.
author: @nataliemccarthy9140

body: Gente! Y es muy fácil de creserlas  en tu jardín se dan en cualquier. Planten su parra les va a encantar además sirve para dar privacidad a tu casa porque Crese en enredaderas de 10 a 15 pies de largas  y como 8 pues de altura ! bendiciones y suerte . Saluditos desde El Paso Texas.
